notes: Antigravity long-idle survival, UNBLOCKED by PID binding via history.jsonl. agy writes the conversationId<->workspace pairing into history.jsonl only on a conversation's SECOND prompt (LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-20: the first-prompt line carries workspace but no conversationId), so the conv-id session cannot bind pid>0 until turn 2. The recipe therefore warms up with two turns BEFORE idling: on turn 2 the next activity refresh re-resolves the real workspace cwd (RefreshOnActivity -> ExtractCWDFromAntigravityHistory) and the pid=0 retry (processActivityLocked:408) binds the live agy PID. With pid>0 bound, the readyTTL sweep SKIPS the session while its process is alive (pid_manager.go:790-794), so it survives the >15s idle and resumes on the next prompt. The daemon also mints a transient proc-<pid> presession at launch; once the conv-id session binds the SAME pid (the pid_discovered for the conv-id), the presession is cleaned up (its transcript_removed events do NOT touch the conv-id session). Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15000ms idle floor cleanly exceeds the TTL. Model is harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE>; this account reports 'Gemini 3.1 Pro'. Tokens/cost are NOT in the antigravity JSONL.

text: The process scanner mints a transient proc-<pid> presession in ready when the agy process is seen at launch
Conv-id-keyed session appears in ready once agy writes transcript.jsonl (a distinct, new session id from the presession)
First user prompt (USER_INPUT) drives the conv-id session ready -> working
Terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls settles the conv-id session back to ready

Warm-up turn 2: ready -> working. This second prompt makes agy write the conversationId<->workspace pairing into history.jsonl, which lets the daemon re-resolve the real cwd and bind pid>0 to the conv-id session
Warm-up turn 2 completes back to ready; the pid>0 bind has now landed for the conv-id session

Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL — the pid>0 liveness check holds the bound conv-id session in ready; state remains ready during the idle (the presession's transcript_removed is a different session and does not fire this invariant)
Next user prompt after the idle: ready -> working resumes normally on the SAME conv-id session
Resume turn completes back to ready; final session state is ready
